prose:
**Benzaldehyde Fukui Reactivity Indices** — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fit) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0 multiplicity 1 (neutral singlet reference; cation +1/doublet and anion −1/doublet for N±1 states). Geometry: user-supplied `.xyz`, single-point (not re-optimized). Mulliken partial charge scheme. SCF converged for all three states; all internal convergence checks passed (charge conservation f⁺ and f⁻ both summed to 1.0).

**Key Results:**
- **Most electrophilic site (f⁺ max):** Atom 3 (ortho C on benzene ring), f⁺ = 0.3994
- **Most nucleophilic site (f⁻ max):** Atom 1 (carbonyl O), f⁻ = 0.2384
- All Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor (Δf) computed for all 14 atoms
- Visualization plot saved to agent output directory

Interpretation: The carbonyl oxygen is the primary nucleophilic center (f⁻ = 0.24). The ortho and para positions on the aromatic ring show the highest electrophilic indices (f⁺ ≈ 0.34–0.40), indicating preference for nucleophilic aromatic substitution at these sites. The ipso carbon (atom 2, directly bonded to the carbonyl) has strongly negative electrophilicity (f⁺ = −0.35), consistent with the electron-withdrawing character of the aldehyde group.

**Method:** DFT, B3LYP functional with def2-TZVP basis set (tier standard), density-fitted integrals, PySCF backend. No warnings reported; integrity status trustworthy.</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.39938966
